preamble: The voiceless uvular stop [q] is a peripheral sound prone to weakening or complete elision, particularly in unstressed syllables or word-final positions.

examples.note: In many modern Arabic dialects, the historical uvular stop is weakened to a glottal stop or lost entirely in certain contexts.
Historical lenition where word-final uvular stops were lost or vocalized into glides.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
